body.title: cc_task_listener not started — Granny dispatches land in channel with no worker picking them up
body.result: TaskListener started as daemon thread inside GrannyDaemon.start(); stop() wired to GrannyDaemon.stop(); stop event replaces bare while True in run()
body.description:
cc_task_listener.py polls for GRANNY_DISPATCH channel messages and marks tickets in_progress via cc_queue dispatch. It exists and works (9 tests pass) but is never started anywhere — no launcher, no superclaude hook, nothing. Granny correctly routes tickets and posts GRANNY_DISPATCH, but without the listener running those dispatches go unacknowledged and tickets sit in sprint status indefinitely.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_task_listener.py, devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** orchestration-over-monolith — listener is logically coupled to GrannyDaemon (Granny dispatches, listener acknowledges); start them together
**Scope boundary:** IN — start TaskListener as a background thread inside GrannyDaemon.start(); stop it in GrannyDaemon.stop(); OUT — do not make CC sprint autonomously (listener only marks in_progress + posts ACK; human still triggers /sprint)
**Completion criteria:** after `python3 -m devices.granny.daemon` starts, a GRANNY_DISPATCH message posted to the channel results in a GRANNY_ACK reply and the ticket status changing to in_progress within one poll cycle (≤15s)
